name: Llama-3.1-Nemotron-Ultra-253B v1 (Reasoning Off)
license: NVIDIA Open Model License + Llama 3.1 Community License Agreement
archDisplay: Dense Transformer, NAS-compressed from Llama-3.1-405B-Instruct (variable FFN + skip-attention + FFN fusion)
note: Flagship open reasoning model derived from Llama-3.1-405B; briefly the most intelligent open model, edging DeepSeek-R1 at under half the size.
tpsNote: Needs ~127GB+ at INT4 — far beyond a single RTX 4090; designed to fit an 8xH100 node.

results.notes: BFCL V2 Live score for the 'Reasoning Off' configuration, from the same official HF model card evaluation table as the corpus's existing MATH500/AIME25/GPQA/IFEval/LiveCodeBench Reasoning-Off scores (all 5 of which were re-confirmed to match exactly: 80.40/16.67/56.60/88.85/29.03). Mapped to the 'bfcl' slug (not 'bfcl-v3') since the card specifies 'V2 Live'.

verificationNotes: Confirmed real via HF model card fetch: 253B params, NVIDIA Open Model License, 128K ctx, released 2025-04-07, all 5 benchmark rows (MATH500 80.40, AIME25 16.67, GPQA 56.60, IFEval 88.85, LiveCodeBench 29.03) match the card exactly. CORRECTED price.provider from 'DeepInfra' to 'NVIDIA API' — could not confirm DeepInfra offers this model (not found on deepinfra.com/nemotron or artificialanalysis.ai provider list, which shows only Nebius Base at $0.60/$1.80); the $2/$6 figure matches NVIDIA's own API rate per InferenceBench.
